data.title: NWPX Infra Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >NWPX
data.description:
NWPX Infrastructure Inc. (NWPX) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 29, 2026.

On April 29, 2026, NWPX Infrastructure, Inc. (the "Company") issued a press release announcing its financial results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 and its current outlook. The press release contains forward-looking statements regarding the Company, and includes cautionary statements identifying important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated. The press release issued April 29, 2026 is furnished herewith as Exhibit No. 99.1 to this Report, and shall not be deemed filed for purposes of Section 18 of the Exchange Act.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1001385/000143774926013887/nwpx20260319_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1001385/000143774926013887/0001437749-26-013887-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Adecoagro announces the filing of its form 20-F for fiscal year 2025
data.description:
LUXEMBOURG, April 29, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Adecoagro S.A. (the "Company") , a leading sustainable production company in South America, hereby announces the filing of its Form 20-F for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC").

The Company's Form 20-F can be accessed by visiting either the SEC's website at www.sec.gov or the "Investors" section of the Company's website at www.adecoagro.com. In addition, shareholders may receive a hard copy of the Company's audited financial statements, or its complete 2025 Form 20-F including audited financial statements, free of charge, by requesting a copy from the investor relations team.

For questions please contact:

Victoria Cabello

IR Officer

Email: ir@adecoagro.com

Additional information about the Company can be found in the "Investors" section on the website at www.adecoagro.com.

About Adecoagro:

Adecoagro is a leading sustainable production company in South America. Adecoagro owns 210.4 thousand hectares of farmland, and several industrial facilities spread across the most productive regions of Argentina, Brazil and Uruguay, where it produces 1.3 million tons of fertilizers, 3.1 million tons of agricultural products and over 1 million MWh of renewable electricity.

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/adecoagro-announces-the-filing-of-its-form-20-f-for-fiscal-year-2025-302757911.html

SOURCE Adecoagro S.A.

data.description:

data.title: Alkami Technology Q1 adj EBITDA nearly doubles on client expansion
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. cloud banking platform's Q1 revenue rose 29% yr/yr, slightly beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 nearly doubled yr/yr to $22.3 mln

  • Company announced $100 mln share repurchase program

Outlook

  • Alkami sees Q2 revenue between $128.0 mln and $129.0 mln

  • Company expects Q2 adjusted EBITDA of $17.9 mln to $18.7 mln

  • Alkami forecasts 2026 revenue of $527.1 mln to $530.9 mln

Result Drivers

  • CLIENT EXPANSION - Co said it expanded its client portfolio, signing 6 new digital banking clients and 14 new MANTL clients in the quarter

  • DIGITAL PLATFORM ADOPTION - Half of new client wins in Q1 were for the Digital Sales & Service Platform, reflecting demand for integrated digital banking solutions

  • USER AND REVENUE GROWTH - Registered users rose by 2.5 mln to 23 mln and revenue per registered user increased 9% yr/yr, supporting higher annual recurring revenue

data.title: Amazon Earnings Beat Estimates. The Stock Is Falling Anyway. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Amazon.com investors will have a lot to look for when the e-commerce giant reports earnings on Wednesday, including cloud growth and impacts from the war in Iran.

Amazon is scheduled to report first-quarter earnings after the stock market closes. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect the company will post adjusted earnings of $1.63 a share, which would be up from earnings of $1.59 a share in the same period a year ago. Revenue for the quarter is estimated to be $177.3 billion, a rise from last year's $155.7 billion.

Amazon is spending big on artificial intelligence, and that's worried investors. The stock dropped after the company reported fourth-quarter financials in February in response to management officials announcing they expect to spend $200 billion on AI in 2026. Shareholders will be watching closely to see if Amazon plans to add to those estimates.

First-quarter purchases of property and equipment, one metric to watch to help gauge Amazon's capital spending, is expected to be $43.6 billion, a jump from last year's $25 billion.

Amazon Web Services is the company's cloud segment. Revenue growth there is key as investors look for proof that companies are seeing returns on their massive AI spending, which is impacting free cash flows. AWS revenue is expected to be $36.6 billion, a 25% jump from the prior year.

Wall Street will also be looking for updates on Amazon's partnership with another AI start-up, OpenAI. Microsoft and OpenAI said in a joint statement on Monday that they no longer have an exclusive partnership. Then on Tuesday, Amazon and OpenAI announced an expansion to their own partnership.

Amazon is also the world's largest online retailer. Shareholders closely watch consumer spending patterns to see if there will be an impact on Amazon sales. The war in Iran has accelerated concerns about consumer sentiment as oil prices rise, impacting inflation and potentially leading to higher for longer interest rates. Data show that consumer spending is still healthy, though. The rising cost of oil, however, could have an impact on Amazon's margins.

"While our e-commerce estimates remain unchanged we contemplate higher operating (namely shipping) costs," UBS analyst Stephen Ju wrote on April 23. He rates Amazon as a Buy with a $304 price target.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: LSB Industries Q1 net sales rise, return to profit
data.description:
Overview

  • US fertilizer producer's Q1 net sales rose yr/yr on higher prices and volumes

  • Q1 net income swung to profit from loss a year earlier

  • Company cites tight market conditions and supply constraints as drivers of improved results

Outlook

  • Company says industrial business is strong with positive market conditions and constrained AN supply

  • Company says fertilizer markets are tight due to the conflicts in the Middle East and pricing remains strong due to global supply disruptions

  • LSB expects El Dorado CCS project operations to begin in Q4 '26 or Q1 '27

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER PRICES & VOLUMES - Increased AN and nitric acid prices and volumes drove higher net sales, per company

  • TIGHT MARKET CONDITIONS - Supply constraints and strong demand in North America led to higher AN selling prices and tight market conditions, according to company

  • SHIFT IN PRODUCT MIX - Production shifted toward AN due to market conditions, resulting in lower UAN sales volumes and slight impact on ammonia sales as inventory was built ahead of a scheduled turnaround

data.title: MYR Group Q1 revenue beats analyst estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US specialty electrical contractor's Q1 revenue rose, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 rose to $2.99

  • Gross margin improved to 13.4% on higher project margins and productivity gains

Outlook

  • Company expects favorable market outlook to support growth through the remainder of 2026

Result Drivers

  • SEGMENT REVENUE GROWTH - T&D revenue rose due to increases in unit price and T&E contracts, while C&I revenue grew mainly from more fixed price contracts

  • HIGHER PROJECT MARGINS - Gross margin improved due to a larger portion of projects progressing at higher contractual margins, better-than-anticipated productivity, favorable change orders and a favorable job closeout

  • RECORD BACKLOG - Backlog reached $2.84 bln, up 7.7% year-over-year, reflecting ongoing demand and expanded market reach
